body: @WindowsAddict Is there a reason why this pull request won't be merged?
author: Aholicknight

body:
> @WindowsAddict Is there a reason why this pull request won't be merged?

Those sandbox analysis reports are very generic, sometimes kind of stupid. For example, if you add -c in the PowerShell command, they will say it's suspicious, but If I just obsfucate it, like set "nil=" &powershell -%nil%c they won't complain.
author: WindowsAddict

body: Added instead https://github.com/massgravel/Microsoft-Activation-Scripts/commit/f551f6199887d4cc9f7e662108156d288fa0692a
author: WindowsAddict

body:
@paolotormon 
PowerShell one-liner command was added to avoid,
- using zip/7z files
- browsers such as chrome warnings when .cmd is inside the .zip file

If users are going to download zip and launch a cmd file, then they can launch MAS AIO cmd file as well. Hence I don't find the need to add that separate cmd file for powershell one liner.
author: WindowsAddict

body: No need to add that.

body:
Hi @Pyenb 
Thanks for the PR, but unfortunately I would have to decline this request because of the following reasons:
- The main focus of this project is on Activation, and if we start doing things like removing ads then there would be a request for Windows ads as well followed by block telemetry and so on and that will bloat the script which I'm trying to avoid.
- Majority of users don't use the outlook app, they use it in the browser, and most already have an adblocker there.
- Antivirus's do not like a script modifying hosts file.
author: WindowsAddict

Hi,

body:
Sorry, but I've to deny this. Extra spacing in the name "Microsoft Activation Scripts" is there because some AV's were/are detecting this string in a file and flagging it. 
PS1 codetag is not used because I don't want to extra highlight those commands. Though I'll take care of full stops, etc in the next MAS update.
author: WindowsAddict

body: The notes section is separate and the main steps to launch MAS have less info because I want to keep that section simple to read and follow. Sorry, have to deny this PR.

author: WindowsAddict

body:
There's several severe issues with this PR anyway. 

UMSKT code is being used in a way where it blatantly violates the GPLv3 license. There's a random exe embedded at the end of the script with a modified version of UMSKT which you haven't made the code for public.

This PR also depends on the Internet Archive for sourcing some files which is, for obvious reasons, unideal. 

PR also automatically installs things which is also unideal for equally obvious reasons. It is much preferred if the users make these changes to their system themselves. MAS prides itself on not modifying the OS beyond corruption fixes
author: thecatontheceiling

body:
Using another service for DoH won't make a difference and it's possible the DoH command hasn't helped in general since we switched our domains to GitHub Pages. 
Also you can't just run a batch script with iex, this is why get.activated.win has a PowerShell bootstrapper that runs the batch script.
